name: usbmuxd
summary: A socket daemon to multiplex connections from and to iOS devices.

documentation.readme: A socket daemon to multiplex connections from and to iOS devices.
usage_sections: Features: usbmuxd stands for "USB multiplexing daemon". This daemon is in charge of multiplexing connections over USB to an iOS device. To users, it means you can use various applications to interact with your device. To developers, it means you can connect to any listening localhost socke

Usage: The daemon is automatically started by udev or systemd depending on what you have configured upon hotplug of an iOS device and exits if the last device was unplugged. When usbmuxd is running it provides a socket interface at /var/run/usbmuxd that is designed to be compatible with

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the USB multiplexing daemon for iOS category. Fits when the engagement needs: A socket daemon to multiplex connections from and to iOS devices.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A socket daemon to multiplex connections from and to iOS devices.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/libimobiledevice/usbmuxd.git. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `./autogen.sh`. Further invocations from upstream: `./autogen.sh --prefix=/opt/local --without-preflight --without-systemd`; `./autogen.sh --help`. A socket daemon to multiplex connections from and to iOS devices.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases, and licence before use. Operates against the operator's own authorized scope only.

use_cases: When you need USB multiplexing daemon for iOS, use usbmuxd because A socket daemon to multiplex connections from and to iOS devices.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./autogen.sh` as documented upstream for usbmuxd.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./autogen.sh --prefix=/opt/local --without-preflight --without-systemd` as documented upstream for usbmuxd.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./autogen.sh --help` as documented upstream for usbmuxd.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `usbmuxd --help` as documented upstream for usbmuxd.
When chaining USB multiplexing daemon for iOS tools, pair usbmuxd with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `usbmuxd --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
